title: A steep dose-response
author: OpenDiscover
blurb: Almost every drug obeys a sigmoid: nothing happens, then over a narrow window of concentration the effect switches from off to full. The Hill equation E = Emax·Cⁿ/(EC50ⁿ+Cⁿ) draws it, with EC50 the concentration for half-maximal effect (here 50) and the Hill coefficient n its steepness. n=2 means cooperativity — the curve snaps rather than eases, so a small dose change flips the response. This is the shape behind potency, therapeutic windows, and IC50 tables.
